_inspectorSnowReport.Report:
Saturday February 28 | 5:44

Bonjour Tremblant,

Happy Saturday! We received 3 cm of fresh snow in the last 24 hours, giving the mountain a nice refresh to start the day!

This morning, we’re also waking up to strong winds on the mountain, so be sure to layer up accordingly and dress for changing conditions. Temperatures are mild today, peaking around 2°C, and conditions are expected to improve as the day goes on. More specifically, skies should clear up a bit this afternoon, with winds gradually lowering, thus setting us up for a beautiful second half of the day on the slopes.

Please note that the Duncan lift will remain closed for maintenance today. Some terrain parks will also open progressively throughout the morning so be sure to stay updated by checking the Conditions page before heading out.

Good news! The Versant Edge will offer extended hours today and will be exceptionally open until 3:30 p.m.

First Tracks Access
Early access begins at 7:45 a.m. via:
North Side: Expo Express and Lowell Thomas
South Side: Télécabine 
Soleil Side: Soleil

Have a fantastic day on the slopes, and ski safe! 

Mountain safety tip of the week: Ski and Ride with Caution.
Skiers and snowboarders should be aware that trail difficulty ratings (green circle, blue square, black diamond, and double black diamond) are not necessarily the same from one mountain to another. The rating system is relative and applies only to this mountain. When visiting a new mountain, skiers and riders of all ability levels should start on the easiest trails and gradually progress as they become familiar with the terrain.

Plan Your Runs
  • Choose trails that match your ability level.
  • Accidents can occur when skiers and snowboarders find themselves on terrain beyond their skill level. Before heading down the mountain, plan your route using a trail map.
Snow fall is recorded daily at 5 a.m from mid-mountain at North Side (550 m / 1,800 ft). This data, based on 24 hours, provides a picture of on-mountain conditions. Actual accumulation may differ from the official report. Be aware of changing conditions. Lifts and trails exist in a mountain environment and may close due to weather, usage and/or special maintenance.
_inspectorSnowReport.Alert:
Please note that the Duncan Express chairlift will be closed today
Work has progressed, but some steps are continuing today with support from our teams, specialized partners and Doppelmayr. The lift will remain closed today, Saturday, February 28th. We appreciate your understanding as our team completes necessary work, and we look forward to reopening it soon.
_inspectorSnowReport.SafetyReport: Today at Tremblant, 3 cm of fresh snow refreshed the slopes overnight. Strong morning winds are expected, with milder temperatures reaching 2°C and gradual improvement this afternoon as skies clear and winds ease. The Duncan lift remains closed for maintenance. Some terrain parks will open progressively, please check the Conditions page for updates. Extended hours today at Versant Edge, open until 3:30 p.m.
_inspectorSnowReport.SafetyReportFrench: Aujourd’hui à Tremblant, 3 cm de neige fraîche ont rafraîchi les pistes durant la nuit. Des vents forts sont prévus ce matin, avec un maximum de 2 °C et une amélioration graduelle cet après-midi alors que le ciel se dégagera et les vents diminueront. Le télésiège Duncan demeure fermé pour entretien. Certains parcs ouvriront progressivement—consultez la page des conditions. Le Versant Edge sera ouvert exceptionnellement jusqu’à 15 h 30.
